it:55.2: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
it:55.3:
"There are these three searches. Which three? The search for sensuality, the search for becoming, the search for a holy life. These are the three searches."
Sensual search, becoming-search, together with the holy-life search — i.e., grasping at truth based on an accumulation of viewpoints:
it:55.4: Through the relinquishing of searches and the abolishing of viewpoints of one dispassionate to all passion, and released in the ending of craving, through the ending of searches, the monk is devoid of perplexity and desire.
it:55.5: This, too, was the meaning of what was said by the Blessed One, so I have heard.
